query: I have a tabular fraud classifier deployed against a fixed database schema. Every incoming record has the same set of columns and every record maps to one of 5 known fraud-type classes. Help me add OOD detection.
expected_behavior: Identifies the deployment as closed-world (fixed schema, finite known classes) and does NOT immediately proceed to set up an OOD scoring method as if the deployment were open-world

Names a more appropriate alternative for this setting — such as training an explicit 'unknown' / 'other' class, a confidence-threshold abstain mechanism for low-margin predictions, or data-drift monitoring over time
Does NOT force the OOD evaluation workflow (AUROC on synthetic OOD samples, FPR-at-95-TPR, near/far OOD curation) onto a setting where there is no genuine novelty class to detect
